subsections.title: Highlight Cards
content: - REQ >95% significantly reduces ineffective conversations: Its proactive follow-up capability is clearly stronger than general-purpose models
- Requirements clarified in <=3 turns on average: Single-session resolution efficiency improves by 70%
- Task resolution rate >=90%: Users are more likely to get actionable recommendation results
- Startup cost reduced by about 80%: Can be implemented with low-code workflows and lightweight models

subsections.title: 2.1 Overview
content: **Business Background**: In recent years, with the explosion of AI technology and the wave of intelligent transformation across industries, massive numbers of non-algorithm practitioners in vertical industries urgently want to find AI tools that meet their needs and suit them, but it is difficult to achieve this through traditional methods. In response to this "information asymmetry" in vertical industries, this example builds an intelligent guidance assistant for selecting AI tools in vertical domains. Using an "expert consultation" style, it proactively asks clarifying follow-up questions in response to vague or incomplete natural-language queries from users, such as "I want to find a tool for writing documents," and gradually delivers precise AI tool recommendations through a limited number of dialogue turns.

subsections.title: 2.2 Classification Tags
content: **Industry**: Information Technology, Science & Education
**Category**: Service, Operations
**Technology Category**: Agent, Large Language Model (LLM), Natural Language Processing (NLP), Knowledge Graph (KG), Intent Understanding, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

subsections.title: 2.3 Implementation Cycle
content: - Demo development: <= 7 days

subsections.title: 2.4 Team Composition
content: - Demo phase: 1 business expert, 1 algorithm engineer

subsections.title: 2.5 Business Pain Points
content: - Vague intent leads to serious hallucinations: Users often cannot accurately describe technical constraints such as budget, deployment method, and specific requirements. Traditional chatbots are either not flexible enough or make blind guesses and recommendations, causing mismatched results, such as recommending expensive enterprise software to individual users, with low usability and confidence

- Low efficiency of passive interaction: When traditional chatbots encounter inputs outside their preset range, they tend to output generic refusal text such as "insufficient information" and lack constructive guidance, forcing users to repeatedly probe and refine their wording before the bot can clarify their needs

- Poor conversion and user experience: Traditional chatbots mostly respond passively and lack expert-level proactive follow-up capability, so conversations are easily interrupted and user churn is high, making it hard to create effective business conversion and cross-recommendation opportunities

subsections.title: 2.6 Core Functions
content: - Follow-up guidance: When users express needs vaguely, the assistant does not answer directly but instead refines and clarifies the user's real needs through 1-3 rounds of follow-up questions

subsections.title: 3.1.1 Reasons for Best Practice Selection
content: - More proactive: REQ >95%, enabling it to consistently initiate effective follow-up questions when information is missing.
- Faster resolution: Follow-up accuracy >92%, with clarification completed in <=3 turns on average, improving conversation efficiency by about 70%.
- More stable experience: Task solution rate >=90% and first-token latency <5 seconds, making it suitable for real-time consultation scenarios.
- Lower cost: Can be implemented with a low-code platform plus lightweight models, reducing startup cost by about 80%.

subsections.title: 3.1.4 Metrics
content: **Core Business Metrics**
- Average conversation turns <= 3: Reduce ineffective conversations and improve the user inquiry process by 70%
- Task Solution Rate (TSR) >= 90%: The proportion of users who ultimately receive satisfactory recommendations, measured based on actions taken such as link clicks or positive ratings
- First-token response latency < 5 seconds
**Core Technical Metrics**
- Task Solution Rate (TSR) >= 90%: Same as the core business metric
- First-token response latency < 5 seconds: Same as the core business metric

- Request Rate (REQ) > 95%: How often the assistant proactively initiates follow-up questions when faced with queries missing information (for GPT-4o, this value is about 15%), helping improve the task solution rate (see core business metric)

- Follow-up Accuracy (Q-ACC) > 92%: The accuracy with which the assistant's follow-up questions hit the core missing information; higher accuracy improves the user inquiry process efficiency (see core business metric)
